html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 甄异传 - Title of the text; can be translated as 'Records of Strange Events' or 'Tales of the Unusual.'
  • 司马谯王 - Title and name of a specific individual; refers to a member of the Sima clan who held the title of King of Qiao during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 像州人 - Refers to someone from Xiangzhou, which could be a specific region or a person's origin.
  • 渭银钏乙只并镜于面 - Cryptic phrase likely meaning 'he wore silver bracelets (or possibly a silver mirror) and other ornaments on his face.' The exact meaning of '乙只' is unclear and may be a transliteration of a term or name.
  • 市酒肉 - Means 'bought wine and meat,' indicating a market or commercial activity.
  • 夜梦道人诉谯王求钏等 - Means 'at night, he dreamed of a Taoist priest who complained to King Qiao about seeking the bracelets (or other items).'
  • 检校即还 - Means 'he checked and returned them immediately.'
  • 书钞一百三十六 - Refers to a specific volume or section of a larger collection of texts, possibly a compilation of historical or anecdotal records.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 司马谯王 - The Sima clan was a powerful family during the Three Kingdoms and Jin periods, and 'King of Qiao' was a title held by members of this clan. The specific individual referred to here is not well-documented, but the context suggests he was a notable figure.
  • 像州 - The exact location of Xiangzhou is not specified, but it was likely a region in China during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 道人 - Refers to a Taoist priest or practitioner, indicating a religious or spiritual context.
  • 钏 - A type of bracelet or ornament, often made of precious metals or stones.
  • 书钞 - A term used in ancient Chinese literature to refer to a collection of texts or a compilation of historical records. '书钞一百三十六' likely refers to a specific volume or section of such a collection.

Proper Nouns

  • 司马谯王 - The title and name of a specific individual.
  • 像州 - A specific region or place.
  • 道人 - A term referring to a Taoist priest or practitioner.
  • 钏 - A type of bracelet or ornament.
  • 书钞一百三十六 - A specific volume or section of a larger collection of texts.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 建武二年 - Second year of the Jianwu era, a specific period in the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 足病不能复去 - Literally 'foot disease cannot move away,' describes a state of being unable to move due to illness.
  • 聊相试耳 - Means 'just testing you' or 'for your trial.'
  • 被命而来 - Means 'coming by command' or 'sent by order.'
  • 不自由 - Means 'not free' or 'not by one's own will.'
  • 赤摽摽其头 - Means 'to mark the head with a red brand.'
  • 小铍刺其心 - Means 'to pierce the heart with a small knife.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 建武 - Reign title of Emperor Guangwu of Han, the founder of the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 张闿 - A historical figure, possibly a government official or scholar.
  • 北台使 - Refers to a messenger or envoy from the north, possibly from the imperial court.
  • 光禄大夫 - A high-ranking official position in the Han dynasty, equivalent to a senior minister or advisor.
  • 广记 - Refers to a collection of stories or anecdotes, often used in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 张闿 - A historical figure, possibly a government official or scholar.
  • 北台使 - Refers to a messenger or envoy from the north, possibly from the imperial court.
  • 光禄大夫 - A high-ranking official position in the Han dynasty, equivalent to a senior minister or advisor.
  • 广记 - Refers to a collection of stories or anecdotes, often used in classical Chinese literature.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 為蘇峻賊軍王免所掠 - Passive construction indicating 'was taken by the rebel army of Su Jun, Wang Mian.' In Classical Chinese, the agent of the action is introduced after the verb, and the passive voice is often implicit.
  • 為奴於東陽蔣鳳家 - 'Was made a slave in the household of Jiang Feng of Dongyang.' The structure '為...於...' is used to indicate the status or role of the subject.
  • 常行山中 - 'Often walked through the mountains.' '行' here means to walk or travel, and '山中' is 'in the mountains.'
  • 見虎檻中狗 - 'Saw a dog in the tiger's cage.' '檻' refers to a cage or enclosure, often used for animals.
  • 竊念狗餓 - 'Secretly thought the dog was hungry.' '竊念' is an idiom meaning 'secretly thought' or 'to oneself thought.'
  • 入檻，方見虎 - 'Entered the cage, and only then saw the tiger.' This shows a sequence of events: entering the cage and then realizing the tiger was inside.
  • 攀木仰看 - 'Climbed a tree and looked up.' '攀木' is climbing a tree, and '仰看' is looking up.
  • 汝不殺我，我放汝 - 'You did not kill me, so I will release you.' This is a direct speech statement, reflecting the narrator's reasoning.
  • 乃開檻出虎 - 'Then opened the cage and released the tiger.' '乃' indicates the result of the previous action.
  • 賊平之後 - 'After the rebels were defeated.' '賊平' is a set phrase meaning 'the rebels were quelled' or 'the rebellion ended.'
  • 允詣縣，別良善 - 'Xu allowed to go to the county office, distinguishing the good from the bad.' '別良善' can mean 'to distinguish the virtuous from the wicked' or 'to separate the good from the bad.'
  • 烏程令張球不為申理 - 'The county magistrate Zhang Qiu of Wucheng did not plead his case.' '申理' means to argue or plead a case, and '不為申理' is a passive construction.
  • 桎梏考楚 - 'Imprisoned and subjected to harsh punishment.' '桎梏' is shackles or restraints, and '考楚' is a term for corporal punishment or torture.
  • 廣記引作考訊 - 'The *Guangji* (Comprehensive Records) cites it as *kao xun* (interrogation).' This is a reference to a later source (*Guangji*), and '考訊' is a term for formal interrogation.
  • 無不至 - 'Without limit' or 'to the extreme.' It modifies the previous verb, indicating the severity of the punishment.
  • 允夢見人雲 - 'Xu dreamed of a person saying...' '雲' is a classical term for 'said' or 'spoke.'
  • 此中易入難出 - 'This place is easy to enter but hard to leave.' A metaphor for the prison or the situation.
  • 汝有慈心，當相拯拔 - 'Because you have a compassionate heart, I will rescue you.' '拯拔' is to save or rescue.
  • 覺見一少年，通身黃衣 - 'Awakened to see a young man, fully dressed in yellow.' '通身' is 'entire body' or 'fully.'
  • 時進獄中與允言語 - 'At times would enter the prison and speak with Xu.' '進獄中' is entering the prison, and '與允言語' is speaking with Xu.
  • 獄吏知是異人 - 'The jailer knew he was a supernatural being.' '異人' is a person of supernatural or extraordinary ability.
  • 由是不敢枉允 - 'Therefore, he dared not wrong Xu.' '枉' here means to wrong or treat unjustly.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 歷陽謝允，字道通 - 'Xie Yun of Lichang, style name Dao Tong.' Lichang (歷陽) is a place name, likely referring to the ancient county of Lichang, which was in present-day Anhui Province. '字道通' is the style name (zi) of the person, which is a common practice in Chinese naming conventions.
  • 蘇峻賊軍 - 'The rebel army of Su Jun.' This refers to the rebellion led by Su Jun (327–329 CE) during the Eastern Jin Dynasty. This was a significant rebellion against the Jin court.
  • 王免 - 'Wang Mian.' This is a name of a subordinate or commander in Su Jun's army. The name is not widely known in historical records, so it is treated as a proper noun.
  • 東陽蔣鳳 - 'Jiang Feng of Dongyang.' Dongyang is a place name, likely referring to a county in Zhejiang Province. Jiang Feng is the name of the household owner.
  • 虎檻 - 'Tiger's cage.' This refers to a cage or enclosure for tigers, which were sometimes kept in or near villages for various purposes, including as symbols of power or for protection.
  • 賊平 - 'The rebels were defeated.' This refers to the suppression of Su Jun's rebellion in 329 CE.
  • 烏程令張球 - 'County magistrate Zhang Qiu of Wucheng.' Wucheng (烏程) is a county, likely in present-day Zhejiang. Zhang Qiu is the name of the magistrate.
  • 廣記引 - 'The *Guangji* (Comprehensive Records) cites it as...' This refers to the *Guangji* (廣記), a compilation of strange stories and anecdotes from the Song Dynasty, edited by Li Fang. It is a well-known source of classical Chinese tales.
  • 考訊 - 'Interrogation.' This is a legal term used in ancient China for formal questioning of suspects.
  • 異人 - 'Supernatural being.' This is a common term in classical Chinese for someone with extraordinary powers or divine origins.
  • 獄吏 - 'Jailer.' This refers to the official in charge of a prison or jail.

Proper Nouns

  • 歷陽 - A county in present-day Anhui Province.
  • 謝允 - A person's name; '允' is the given name, and '道通' is the style name.
  • 蘇峻 - A rebel leader during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 王免 - A commander or subordinate in Su Jun's army.
  • 東陽 - A county in present-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 蔣鳳 - The owner of the household where Xie Yun was enslaved.
  • 烏程 - A county in present-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 張球 - The county magistrate of Wucheng.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 獄吏以告令長 - The prison official informed the magistrate.
  • 令長由是不敢诬辱 - The magistrate, therefore, dared not defame or humiliate [the person].
  • 蒙理还 - After being cleared.
  • 太尉庾公亮 - Grand Commandant Yu Gongliang.
  • 给其资粮 - Provided him with provisions.
  • 遂到襄阳 - He then arrived in Xiangyang.
  • 见道士 - Met a Taoist priest.
  • 吾师戴先生孟盛子 - My master, Master Dai Shengzi.
  • 敕：‘若有西上欲见我者，可将来。’ - Imperative: 'If anyone from the west wishes to see me, bring them.'
  • 得无是君 - Could it be this person?
  • 广记引作吾师戴先生者成人君子尝言有志者与之俱来得非尔邪 - The *Yingyai Shenglan* records: 'My master, Master Dai Shengzi, a virtuous and noble person, once said that those with aspirations should come together. Could it not be you?'
  • 斋戒三日 - Fasted and abstained for three days.
  • 进见先生 - Met the master.
  • 乃昔日所梦人也 - He was the person I had dreamed of before.
  • 欲见黄衣童子否 - Do you wish to see the Yellow Robed Child?
  • 赐以神药三丸 - Gave him three pills of divine medicine.
  • 服之便不饥渴 - Upon taking them, one would no longer feel hungry or thirsty.
  • 无所思欲 - No desires or thoughts.
  • 先生亦无常处 - The master also has no fixed place.
  • 时有祥云紫气荫其上 - At times, auspicious clouds and purple vapor shroud him.
  • 芬馥之气 - A fragrant and pleasant smell.
  • 御览引作或闻香气彻于山谷 - The *Yujian* records: 'Sometimes the fragrance permeates the valleys.'
  • 御览四十三广记四百二十六 - Volume 43 of the *Yujian* and Volume 426 of the *Yingyai Shenglan*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狱吏 - Prison official.
  • 令长 - Magistrate.
  • 太尉 - Grand Commandant.
  • 庾公亮 - Yu Gongliang.
  • 武当山 - Mount Wudang.
  • 襄阳 - Xiangyang.
  • 道士 - Taoist priest.
  • 戴先生孟盛子 - Master Dai Shengzi.
  • 黄衣童子 - Yellow Robed Child.
  • 神药 - Divine medicine.
  • 祥云紫气 - Auspicious clouds and purple vapor.
  • 芬馥之气 - Fragrant and pleasant smell.

Proper Nouns

  • 狱吏 - Prison official.
  • 令长 - Magistrate.
  • 太尉庾公亮 - Grand Commandant Yu Gongliang.
  • 武当山 - Mount Wudang.
  • 襄阳 - Xiangyang.
  • 道士 - Taoist priest.
  • 戴先生孟盛子 - Master Dai Shengzi.
  • 黄衣童子 - Yellow Robed Child.
  • 神药 - Divine medicine.
  • 祥云紫气 - Auspicious clouds and purple vapor.
  • 芬馥之气 - Fragrant and pleasant smell.
  • 御览 - The *Yujian*.
  • 广记 - The *Yingyai Shenglan*.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 登厕 - Euphemism for going to the toilet.
  • 攘臂 - To raise one's arm, often indicating anger or determination.
  • 应手有声 - To make a sound upon contact.
  • 因而寝疾遂亡 - To fall ill and eventually die.
  • 被差为征 - To be conscripted for military service.
  • 杀鸡求福 - To sacrifice a chicken to pray for good fortune.
  • 煮鸡头在柈中 - To cook the chicken head in a tray.
  • 忽然而鸣 - To suddenly make a sound.
  • 破贼 - To defeat the enemy.
  • 引弓射之 - To draw a bow and shoot at it.
  • 正中其喉 - To hit directly in the throat.
  • 奄忽便绝 - To suddenly die.
  • 十余日乃苏 - After more than ten days, he/she woke up.
  • 四肢如被挝损 - Limbs were as if beaten.
  • 犹不分明 - Still not clear.
  • 色甚不美 - Appearance was very unattractive.
  • 本以声见取 - Originally chosen for singing ability.
  • 既被患 - After suffering the illness.
  • 遂不得嫁 - Could not be married.
  • 遽升荣位 - Suddenly rose to a high position.
  • 鸡之祅更为祥 - The omen of the chicken turned into a good sign.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庾亮 - Yu Liang, a prominent official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 荆州 - Jingzhou, one of the nine administrative regions of China during the Han dynasty.
  • 方相 - An ancient mythological creature often depicted with a fearsome appearance.
  • 徐州 - Xuzhou, a city in northern Jiangsu province.
  • 太元 - The era name of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (376–396).
  • 征 - Military service.
  • 金吾 - A high-ranking official responsible for maintaining order in the capital.
  • 司马义 - Jinwu Sima Yi, a high-ranking official.
  • 碧玉 - The name of a concubine.
  • 善弦歌 - Skilled in playing musical instruments and singing.
  • 广记 - A collection of anecdotes and stories.
  • 引作 - A reference to a specific entry.
  • 遽升荣位 - Suddenly rose to a high position.
  • 鸡之祅更为祥 - The omen of the chicken turned into a good sign.
  • 御览 - A comprehensive encyclopedia.
  • 四百六十一 - The specific volume or chapter number.
  • 三百二十一 - The specific volume or chapter number.

Proper Nouns

  • 庾亮 - Yu Liang.
  • 荆州 - Jingzhou.
  • 徐州 - Xuzhou.
  • 太元 - Taiyuan era.
  • 征 - Military service.
  • 金吾 - Jinwu, a high-ranking official.
  • 司马义 - Sima Yi, a high-ranking official.
  • 碧玉 - Bi Yu, a concubine.
  • 善弦歌 - Skill in playing musical instruments and singing.
  • 广记 - Guang Ji, a collection of anecdotes and stories.
  • 引作 - Yin Zuo, a reference to a specific entry.
  • 遽升荣位 - Ju Sheng Rong Wei, suddenly rose to a high position.
  • 鸡之祅更为祥 - Ji Zhi Yao Geng Wei Xiang, the omen of the chicken turned into a good sign.
  • 御览 - Yu Lian, a comprehensive encyclopedia.
  • 四百六十一 - Si Bai Liu Shi Yi, the specific volume or chapter number.
  • 三百二十一 - San Bai Er Shi Yi, the specific volume or chapter number.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忽有鬼来助驱使 - Common idiom in Chinese folklore meaning 'suddenly a ghost came to help with tasks.'
  • 破甑已无所用 - Means 'the broken pot is useless,' indicating something no longer functional.
  • 鬼使撞瓮底穿为甑 - Means 'the ghost made a new pot by drilling the bottom of a jar,' describing a supernatural action.
  • 比家人起，饭已熟 - Means 'by the time the family woke up, the rice was cooked,' indicating the ghost's efficiency.
  • 此鬼无他须，唯啖甘蔗 - Means 'this ghost had no other needs, only ate sugarcane,' describing the ghost's peculiar habit.
  • 林每独见之 - Means 'Zhang Mu often saw it alone,' indicating the ghost's solitary appearance.
  • 形如少女 - Means 'it looked like a young girl,' describing the ghost's appearance.
  • 面青黑色，遍身青衣 - Means 'with a blue-black face, wearing a blue robe all over,' further detailing the ghost's appearance.
  • 乃令林家取一白瓮，盛水半，以绢覆头 - Means 'then he told Zhang Mu's family to get a white jar and fill it halfway with water, cover the top with a piece of silk,' describing the ghost's instructions.
  • 明旦视之，有物在中 - Means 'the next morning when he looked inside, there was something inside,' describing the result.
  • 林家素贫，因此遂富 - Means 'Zhang Mu's family was originally poor, but they became rich because of this,' indicating a change in fortune.
  • 尝语 - Means 'once said,' introducing a statement.
  • 毋恶我，日月尽，自去 - Means 'don't hate me, when the days are over, I will leave,' indicating the ghost's departure.
  • 后果去 - Means 'as expected, he left,' confirming the ghost's departure.
  • 广记三百二十二 - Refers to 'in the *Taiping Guangji*, volume 322,' indicating a source.
  • 御览九百四十七又七百五十八并略 - Refers to 'in the *Taiping Yulan*, volumes 947 and 758, briefly,' indicating additional sources.
  • 并略 - Means 'briefly,' indicating a summary.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴兴 - Refers to Wu County, a region in present-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 隆安 - Refers to the era of Emperor An of Eastern Jin, a period of significant cultural and political change.
  • 御览 - Refers to the *Taiping Yulan* (太平御览), a large compendium of anecdotes compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 广记 - Refers to the *Taiping Guangji* (太平广记), another large compendium of anecdotes compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 古冢 - Refers to ancient tombs, indicating the presence of historical sites.
  • 甘蔗 - Refers to sugarcane, a common food item in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 吴兴张牧 - Zhang Mu from Wu County.
  • 东乡杨里 - Yang Village, East Township.
  • 隆安 - The era of Emperor An of Eastern Jin.
  • 御览 - The *Taiping Yulan*.
  • 广记 - The *Taiping Guangji*.
  • 高褐 - The name given by the ghost.
  • 阿褐 - The name given by the owner.
  • 林 - Zhang Mu.
  • 林家 - Zhang Mu's family.
  • 古冢 - Ancient tombs.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 沛郡人秦樹御覽引作拊下同者 - Indicates that the name '秦樹' is also written as '拊' in *Taiping Yulan*, suggesting the two names are used interchangeably.
  • 天暗失道 - Literally means 'the sky darkened and the path was lost,' describing a situation where the protagonist lost his way in the dark.
  • 保无虑，不相误也 - A reassurance that there is no need to worry, and the host will not lead the guest astray.
  • 食物悉是陈久 - Means 'all the food was old and stale,' indicating that the food served was not fresh.
  • 欲结大义，能相顾否 - A formal way of asking if the woman would agree to marry him, using '结大义' to refer to marriage.
  • 鄙薄 - A modest self-deprecation, meaning 'unworthy' or 'insignificant.'
  • 一睹，后面莫期 - A poignant farewell, meaning 'one meeting, no more to come.'
  • 指环一双 - Refers to a pair of finger rings, likely a symbol of commitment or affection.
  • 带结如故 - Means 'the knot in the belt remained as it was,' suggesting that the ring was lost but the knot in the belt (perhaps a symbol of the encounter) remained unchanged.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 沛郡 - A historical county in present-day Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 曲阿小辛村 - A specific village in the region, though its exact location is not widely known today.
  • 义熙中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Gong of Liu Song (刘宋), specifically the year 405 CE, during the Eastern Jin Dynasty. '义熙' is the era name of this period.
  • 御览 - Refers to *Taiping Yulan* (太平御覽), a massive compendium compiled during the Northern Song Dynasty (960–1127).
  • 冢墓 - Refers to a tomb or grave, symbolizing death and the afterlife in Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦树 - The name of the protagonist, also written as '拊' in *Taiping Yulan*.
  • 曲阿小辛村 - The name of the village where the protagonist's family lived.
  • 义熙 - The era name of the reign of Emperor Gong of Liu Song (405 CE).
  • 御览 - *Taiping Yulan* (太平御覽), a large-scale encyclopedic work compiled during the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 冢墓 - Refers to a tomb or grave.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 病死 - Literally 'died of illness,' but in Classical Chinese, it often refers to a sudden or unexpected death.
  • 未殡而苏 - 'Not yet buried but revived,' indicating a resurrection or return to life after death.
  • 被录到天曹 - 'Recorded in the celestial bureaucracy,' referring to being summoned to the afterlife or the realm of the gods.
  • 主者是其外兄 - 'The judge was his maternal uncle,' indicating a familial relationship in the afterlife.
  • 断理得免 - 'Judged and released,' meaning that the judgment was favorable and the person was spared.
  • 见一女同时被录 - 'Saw a woman who was also recorded,' indicating that another person was summoned to the afterlife at the same time.
  • 脱金钏二双 - 'Removed two pairs of gold bracelets,' referring to the act of offering valuables to the judge.
  • 托沈以与主者 - 'Entrusted to Shen to give to the judge,' indicating an intermediary role in the afterlife.
  • 亦得还 - 'Also returned,' meaning that the woman was also spared and returned to life.
  • 遂共宴接 - 'Then they feasted together,' indicating a celebratory gathering after their return.
  • 家在吴 - 'Her home is in Wu,' referring to a specific region in ancient China.
  • 姓徐、名秋英 - 'Her surname is Xu and her given name is Qiu Ying,' providing the woman's full name.
  • 寻问 - 'Inquired,' indicating a search or investigation.
  • 遂得之 - 'Finally found her,' meaning that the woman was located.
  • 父母因以女妻沈 - 'Her parents then married their daughter to Shen,' indicating a marriage arrangement.
  • 病亡 - 'Died of illness,' similar to '病死' but often used in contexts of prolonged illness.
  • 见大山下有十余小儿 - 'Saw more than ten children under a big hill,' indicating a supernatural or dream-like encounter.
  • 共推一大车 - 'Pushing a large cart together,' describing a collective action.
  • 车高数丈 - 'The cart was several zhang tall,' emphasizing the size and strangeness of the cart.
  • 伯远亦推之 - 'Baiyuan also pushed it,' indicating his participation in the action.
  • 天风暴起扬尘 - 'A violent storm suddenly arose, raising dust,' describing a dramatic weather event.
  • 因桑枝而住 - 'Stopped at a mulberry branch,' indicating a pause in the action.
  • 闻呼声 - 'Hearing a shout,' describing an auditory experience.
  • 发中皆有沙尘 - 'There was sand and dust in his hair,' indicating the physical evidence of the storm.
  • 至泰山 - 'Reached Mount Tai,' referring to a significant mountain in Chinese culture.
  • 识桑 - 'Recognized the mulberry,' indicating a familiar object.
  • 如死时所见之也 - 'As he had seen it when he died,' linking the current experience to a previous one.
  • 居彭城 - 'Lived in Pengcheng,' referring to a specific city in ancient China.
  • 遭暴风雨 - 'Encountered a violent storm,' describing a natural disaster.
  • 墙宇破坏 - 'The walls and buildings were destroyed,' indicating the extent of the damage.
  • 泣拥稚子 - 'Cried and hugged the young child,' describing an emotional reaction.
  • 汝爷若在，岂至于此 - 'If your father were alive, how could this happen?' expressing a lament or regret.
  • 夜梦沙将数十人 - 'Dreamed of Shasha leading dozens of people,' indicating a supernatural experience.
  • 料理宅舍 - 'Arranged the house and buildings,' describing a practical action.
  • 明日完矣 - 'They were completed the next day,' indicating the outcome of the dream.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乐安 - A place name in ancient China, possibly referring to a specific region or city.
  • 吴 - A region in ancient China, often associated with the state of Wu.
  • 彭城 - A city in ancient China, known as Pengcheng, located in present-day Xuzhou, Jiangsu Province.
  • 泰山 - Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains of China, often associated with the afterlife and immortality.
  • 御览 - 'Imperial Review,' a collection of imperial notes and records compiled during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 广记 - 'General Records,' a collection of stories and anecdotes compiled during the Song Dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 被录到天曹 can be interpreted as 'recorded in the celestial bureaucracy' or 'summoned to the afterlife,' depending on the context.
  • 主者是其外兄 can be interpreted as 'the judge was his maternal uncle' or 'the judge was related to him,' emphasizing the familial relationship.
  • 脱金钏二双 can be interpreted as 'removed two pairs of gold bracelets' or 'gave two pairs of gold bracelets,' depending on the context.
  • 托沈以与主者 can be interpreted as 'entrusted to Shen to give to the judge' or 'entrusted to the judge to give to Shen,' depending on the context.
  • 遂共宴接 can be interpreted as 'then they feasted together' or 'then they celebrated together,' depending on the context.
  • 家在吴 can be interpreted as 'her home is in Wu' or 'she is from Wu,' depending on the context.
  • 寻问 can be interpreted as 'inquired' or 'searched,' depending on the context.
  • 遂得之 can be interpreted as 'finally found her' or 'finally located her,' depending on the context.
  • 父母因以女妻沈 can be interpreted as 'her parents then married their daughter to Shen' or 'her parents then arranged for their daughter to marry Shen,' depending on the context.
  • 病亡 can be interpreted as 'died of illness' or 'died suddenly,' depending on the context.
  • 见大山下有十余小儿 can be interpreted as 'saw more than ten children under a big hill' or 'saw children under a big hill,' depending on the context.
  • 共推一大车 can be interpreted as 'pushing a large cart together' or 'pushing a cart together,' depending on the context.
  • 车高数丈 can be interpreted as 'the cart was several zhang tall' or 'the cart was very tall,' depending on the context.
  • 伯远亦推之 can be interpreted as 'Baiyuan also pushed it' or 'Baiyuan also helped push it,' depending on the context.
  • 天风暴起扬尘 can be interpreted as 'a violent storm suddenly arose, raising dust' or 'a storm suddenly arose, raising dust,' depending on the context.
  • 因桑枝而住 can be interpreted as 'stopped at a mulberry branch' or 'rested at a mulberry branch,' depending on the context.
  • 闻呼声 can be interpreted as 'heard a shout' or 'heard a cry,' depending on the context.
  • 发中皆有沙尘 can be interpreted as 'there was sand and dust in his hair' or 'his hair was covered in sand and dust,' depending on the context.
  • 至泰山 can be interpreted as 'reached Mount Tai' or 'arrived at Mount Tai,' depending on the context.
  • 识桑 can be interpreted as 'recognized the mulberry' or 'saw the mulberry,' depending on the context.
  • 如死时所见之也 can be interpreted as 'as he had seen it when he died' or 'as he had seen it before,' depending on the context.
  • 居彭城 can be interpreted as 'lived in Pengcheng' or 'resided in Pengcheng,' depending on the context.
  • 遭暴风雨 can be interpreted as 'encountered a violent storm' or 'experienced a violent storm,' depending on the context.
  • 墙宇破坏 can be interpreted as 'the walls and buildings were destroyed' or 'the buildings were destroyed,' depending on the context.
  • 泣拥稚子 can be interpreted as 'cried and hugged the young child' or 'cried and held the young child,' depending on the context.
  • 汝爷若在，岂至于此 can be interpreted as 'if your father were alive, how could this happen?' or 'if your father were here, how could this happen?' depending on the context.
  • 夜梦沙将数十人 can be interpreted as 'dreamed of Shasha leading dozens of people' or 'dreamed of dozens of people led by Shasha,' depending on the context.
  • 料理宅舍 can be interpreted as 'arranged the house and buildings,' describing a practical action.
  • 明日完矣 can be interpreted as 'they were completed the next day,' indicating the outcome of the dream.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 命召君为主簿 - Formal expression meaning 'to summon you as my chief clerk.'
  • 视天上，当有所见 - Means 'look up at the sky, and you will see something.'
  • 空中见人垂旐罗列 - Describes a scene where people are seen hanging banners in the air, arranged in a procession.
  • 状如送葬 - Means 'like a funeral procession.'
  • 闻语声，不见其形 - Means 'heard his voice but did not see his form.'
  • 家人苦请，求得见之 - Means 'his family persistently asked and finally managed to see him.'
  • 我困瘁未忍见汝 - Means 'I am in a state of exhaustion and cannot bear to see you.'
  • 坐平生时罚挞失道 - Means 'due to the punishment and beating I received in life, which led me astray.'
  • 又杀卒及奴 - Means 'also killing soldiers and slaves.'
  • 以此减算 - Means 'therefore reducing my lifespan.'
  • 去受使到长沙 - Means 'after being sent on a mission to Changsha.'
  • 教其二子 - Means 'instructed his two sons.'
  • 门户沦没 - Means 'our family line is in decline.'
  • 岂是人子 - Means 'is this the behavior of a son?'
  • 责其兄不垂教诲 - Means 'blamed his brother for not imparting teachings.'
  • 孟禺已名配死录 - Means 'Mèng Yú has already been named in the death register.'
  • 正余有日限耳 - Means 'I just have a limited time left.'
  • 气强力壮 - Means 'strong and robust in health.'
  • 后到所期暴亡 - Means 'later died suddenly upon reaching the expected date.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长沙王 - Refers to a historical figure or a regional king in Changsha, indicating a specific time and place.
  • 海盐令 - Indicates the administrative division of Haixian during the period.
  • 主簿 - A high-ranking administrative official in ancient China.
  • 七月十五日 - The 15th day of the seventh month, often associated with the Ghost Festival in Chinese culture.
  • 广陵 - Refers to Guangling, an ancient region in modern-day Yangzhou, Jiangsu Province.
  • 江陵 - Refers to Jiangling, a city in Hubei Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 广记三百二十二 - Indicates the source of the story, likely from a collection of anecdotes or legends.
  • 孟禺 - A specific person mentioned in the text, possibly a family member or acquaintance.

Proper Nouns

  • 长沙王思规 - A historical figure or regional king.
  • 海盐令 - An administrative title.
  • 主簿 - An official title.
  • 广陵华逸 - A historical figure.
  • 江陵 - A city in Hubei Province.
  • 孟禺 - A specific person mentioned in the text.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 亡后一年，见形还家 - One year after his death, his spirit appeared at home. Common in Chinese ghost stories, where the deceased returns to their family.
  • 乘犊车，宾从数十人 - Riding in a small carriage with dozens of attendants. Describes the ghost's appearance, emphasizing his status.
  • 自云北海太守 - He said he was the Governor of Beihai. A formal title indicating his high rank in life.
  • 家设馔，见所饮食，当时皆尽，去后器满如故 - The family prepared food, and he ate everything, but after he left, the containers were still full. Supernatural detail suggesting the ghost did not consume the food physically.
  • 家人号泣，文规曰：“勿哭，寻便来。” - The family cried, but Wen Gui said, 'Don't cry, I will come soon.' Typical interaction in ghost stories, where the ghost reassures the living.
  • 或一月或四五十日辄来，或停半日 - Sometimes he came once a month, sometimes every 40 to 50 days, and sometimes he stayed for half a day. Describes the irregularity of his visits.
  • 其所将赤衣驺导，形皆短小，坐息篱间及厢屋中，不知 - The attendants he brought were all dressed in red and were short in stature, sitting and resting among the hedges and in the side rooms, but no one knew who they were. Supernatural detail, describing the ghost's retinue.
  • 文规当去时，家人每呼令起，玩习不为异物 - When Wen Gui was about to leave, the family would call out to him to get up, and he would playfully ignore them, treating them as ordinary things. Shows the ghost's casual attitude toward the living.
  • 文规有数岁孙，念之，抱来，左右鬼神抱取以进，此儿不堪鬼气，便绝，不复识人 - Wen Gui missed his several-year-old grandson, so he brought him over. The ghostly attendants took the child and brought him forward, but the child couldn't withstand the ghostly presence and died, never recognizing anyone again. Tragic detail, showing the danger of encountering ghosts.
  • 文规索水噀之，乃醒 - Wen Gui fetched water and sprayed it on the child, and the child woke up. Magical detail, showing the ghost's ability to revive the child.
  • 见庭中桃树，乃曰：“此桃我昔所种，子甚美好。” - He saw a peach tree in the courtyard and said, 'These peaches were planted by me long ago, and the fruit is very delicious.' Nostalgic detail, showing the ghost's connection to his past.
  • 人言亡者畏桃，君何为不畏？ - People say that the dead fear peaches, so why don't you? Cultural reference, as peaches are often associated with warding off evil spirits in Chinese folklore.
  • 答曰：“桃东南枝长二尺八寸向日者憎之，或亦不畏。” - He replied, 'I dislike peach branches in the southeast that are two feet eight inches long and facing the sun, but I might not fear them.' Specific cultural belief, indicating that certain types of peach branches are more effective at warding off ghosts.
  • 已上略见类聚八十六御览九百六十七　齐民要术十见地有蒜壳，令拾去之，观其意似憎蒜而畏桃也 - The above is a brief excerpt from *Classical Collections* 86, *Imperial Reader* 967, *Essential Techniques for the Common People* 10, and *Imperial Reader* 967. It is observed that he dislikes garlic but fears peaches. Provides historical context, indicating the source of the story and the cultural beliefs about garlic and peaches.
  • 广记三百二十五 - This is recorded in *Comprehensive Records* 325. Provides the source of the story in a larger collection of ghost stories.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 谯郡 - A historical county in ancient China, located in present-day Anhui Province.
  • 夏侯文规 - A historical figure, likely a noble or official from the Eastern Han period.
  • 北海太守 - A high-ranking official position, indicating that Wen Gui held a significant role in government.
  • 桃 - Peaches are often associated with warding off evil spirits in Chinese folklore, as seen in the story.
  • 蒜 - Garlic is also believed to have protective properties against evil spirits in Chinese culture.
  • 类聚八十六 - Refers to *Classical Collections* 86, a historical text that compiles various classical works.
  • 御览九百六十七 - Refers to *Imperial Reader* 967, another historical text that compiles classical works.
  • 齐民要术十 - Refers to *Essential Techniques for the Common People* 10, a historical text on agriculture and daily life.
  • 广记三百二十五 - Refers to *Comprehensive Records* 325, a large collection of ghost stories and supernatural tales.

  • 蒜 - Garlic, with cultural significance in Chinese folklore.

  • 御览九百六十七 - *Imperial Reader* 967, a historical text.
